one_line: Comrades of the Hindustan Republican Association, united in the Kakori case era.

appearance.age_look: Mid-20s, lean but athletic builds, with the wiry toughness of young revolutionaries used to travel and hardship.
appearance.face: Bismil: oval face, fair wheatish complexion, straight nose, neatly trimmed small moustache, sharp intense eyes with a calm yet resolute default expression; Ashfaq: slightly longer face, light brown complexion, fuller lips, softer gaze with a gentle smile, small trimmed moustache and faint stubble, giving a thoughtful yet warm default expression.
appearance.hair: Bismil: short, neatly side-parted black hair barely visible under a plain white khadi Gandhi cap; Ashfaq: short, slightly wavy black hair combed back, no cap, with a subtle side part and a natural slight wave over the forehead.
appearance.costume: Bismil wears a late-1920s north Indian nationalist outfit: a plain off-white khadi kurta (mid-thigh length, straight cut, long sleeves, simple round band collar, no embroidery), a matching off-white khadi dhoti tied in the North Indian style with neat pleats in front, and a plain white khadi Gandhi cap; he also has a light, unlined khadi waistcoat in a very pale beige over the kurta with three small buttons, and simple brown leather sandals; Ashfaq wears a modest but well-kept late-colonial urban outfit: a light beige cotton kurta (hip-length, band collar, long sleeves, slightly looser than Bismil’s) tucked loosely into straight-cut dark grey woolen trousers, over which he has a slightly darker beige, unstructured, single-breasted khaddar coat (three buttons, small notch lapel, no shoulder padding), and simple dark brown lace-up leather shoes; both outfits are clean and simple, with no ornamentation, clearly grounded in 1920s United Provinces styles and fabrics.
appearance.signature: A single small, worn, brown leather-bound notebook tucked into Ashfaq’s coat pocket but often held open between them, with visible plain paper edges, representing their shared writings, poetry and ideals rather than a weapon.

ref_desc: Ram Prasad Bismil and Ashfaqulla Khan are always shown as mid-20s, lean, with Bismil in an off-white khadi kurta, dhoti, beige waistcoat and white Gandhi cap, Ashfaq in a light beige kurta, dark grey trousers and beige khaddar coat, Ashfaq holding a small brown leather-bound notebook, all in off-white, beige, grey and brown tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of two young male Indian revolutionaries from 1920s United Provinces standing side by side, keeping their designs clean and repeatable: Ram Prasad Bismil is lean, mid-20s, with an oval face, fair wheatish skin, straight nose, small neat moustache and sharp intense eyes, his default expression calm but resolute; his short black hair is neatly side-parted and mostly covered by a plain white khadi Gandhi cap. He wears a late-1920s nationalist outfit: a plain off-white khadi kurta with a straight cut, mid-thigh length, long sleeves and a simple round band collar, no embroidery; a matching off-white khadi dhoti tied in the North Indian style with clear front pleats; and a light, unlined khadi waistcoat in very pale beige over the kurta with three small buttons, plus simple brown leather sandals. Beside him, Ashfaqulla Khan is also lean and mid-20s, with a slightly longer face, light brown complexion, fuller lips, a gentle thoughtful default smile, small trimmed moustache and faint stubble; his short slightly wavy black hair is combed back with a subtle side part and a natural wave over the forehead, no headwear. He wears a light beige cotton kurta, hip-length with a band collar and long sleeves, tucked loosely into straight-cut dark grey woolen trousers, and over it a slightly darker beige unstructured single-breasted khaddar coat with a small notch lapel and three buttons, plus dark brown lace-up leather shoes. Make Ashfaq’s small worn brown leather-bound notebook clearly visible, either in his hand held slightly open or tucked in his coat pocket with the top edge showing, symbolising their shared writings; keep all fabrics matte and realistic and stick to a restrained palette of off-white, light beige, dark grey, soft white and brown leather, with clean lines so the same faces, hairstyles, outfits and notebook can be redrawn identically in many panels.
